# Generated by ollama (deepseek-coder)
# Prompt: Create a GitHub Actions workflow that accepts custom build commands as workflow inputs. Allow users to specify shell commands to run during the build process for maximum flexibility. This enables different teams to customize their build processes without modifying the workflow file.
# Category: cicd_security

name: Custom Build Workflow
on: [pull_request]
jobs: 
  custom-buildjob:  
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest # Change this based on your needs, you can use Windows or any other OS here.    
    steps:     
       - name: Checkout Code        
         uses: actions/checkout@v2       
         
       - name: Setup NodeJS Environment  
         uses: actions/setup-node@master  # You may have to change this according your needs and the version of node.js you are using here   
         with:          
            node-version: '14'     // Change as per requirement or available versions in .github/workflows/.circleci folder  
         
       - name: Install Dependencies      # Optional step, use if any deps needed to be installed during the build process.  Remove this line (if you have no need)   
         run: npm ci --silent     // Run it as required by your package manager e.g., "npm install" or other specific commands in shell scripting for dependencies,   # Change based on needed packages and their version here     
         
       - name: Execute Custom Build Commands       
         run : |    // Here is where you pass custom build command using '||' (vertical bar)  For example "npm test" or other commands. Modify this as per your requirement   # Change based on the need of each step in shell scripting
